inputs.fcf_source: FY2025 cash from operations $2,059M less depreciation and amortization $894M (maintenance capex proxy) = $1,165M ÷ ~195.2M diluted shares
inputs.dividend_source: FY2025 common dividends declared $2.83 per share (10-K XBRL)
inputs.normalization_note: Regulated utility: growth capital spending $3,126M exceeds operating cash flow but is recovered through rate-base growth and infrastructure surcharges. Lawrence uses maintenance-adjusted owner cash (OCF minus depreciation), not GAAP free cash flow.

bear.notes: Regulatory lag, Essential merger delay or break, higher financing costs on $12.8B debt

base.notes: Rate-base growth from $3.1B annual capex; infrastructure surcharge authorizations; standalone AWK pre-Essential close

bull.notes: Faster regulatory recovery; Essential merger synergies; privatization tuck-ins (Nexus-style)

option_scan.question: GAAP book misstates core assets?
option_scan.evidence: Regulated rate-base accounting; PP&E $30.6B net on balance sheet at 12/31/2025

option_scan.question: In-business loss segment?
option_scan.evidence: Market-Based Businesses ~8% of revenue ($417M FY2025); smaller and less regulated than core

option_scan.question: Backlog / RPO not in FCF path?
option_scan.evidence: Revenue recognized as services delivered; infrastructure surcharges flow through regulated tariff

option_scan.question: Private or illiquid stakes below fair value?
Transitory distribution / legal recovery?
option_scan.evidence: Steady regulated dividend; no litigation recovery thesis

option_scan.question: Embedded product option in revenue path?

growth_explanation.mechanism: Regulated rate-base growth from capital investment ($3.1B FY2025 capex) recovered through general rate cases and infrastructure surcharges across 14 state PUCs; organic customer growth and tuck-in acquisitions (e.g. Nexus ~$200M rate base, June 2026).
growth_explanation.source: FY2025 10-K: Regulated Businesses revenue $4,723M (+10% YoY); annualized incremental revenues from rate authorizations in XBRL

essential_merger.notes: Transformative combination; regulatory approvals pending. Base case models standalone AWK owner cash until human approves pro forma blend.

paths.label: Growth theory (pre-falsifier)
paths.weight_why: Segment blend before falsifier pass; diagnostic only when it differs from falsifier-adjusted.
weight_falsifiers: Included at full weight alongside falsifier-adjusted without divergence note

paths.label: Bear scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Downside stress; low weight because bear is a sensitivity, not a competing explanation of base mechanics.
weight_falsifiers: Bear assumptions become base case in filings (e.g. sustained volume collapse)
paths.hard_to_vary: yes: asymmetric low weight is structural

paths.label: Bull scenario (Lawrence)
paths.weight_why: Upside stress probe; higher than bear because optionality archetype has skew, but still below primary paths.
weight_falsifiers: Bull growth sustained in filings for 4+ quarters without falsifier trigger

qualitative_adjustments.factor: No dated predictive attribute at spot
qualitative_adjustments.rationale: Scarcity is descriptive; no timed mispricing catalyst in base
qualitative_adjustments.falsifier: Cross-check names dated catalyst with timeline

deutsch_checks.reach: Weights encode evidence tier (A primary → D external), not fitted to hit a target IRR

synthesis.notes: Capstone IRR combining filings, segments, overlays, third-party, qualitative
